proof_script:
Lemma add_comm : forall n m : nat, n + m = m + n.
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IHn'].
  - simpl. rewrite Nat.add_0_r. reflexivity.
  - simpl. rewrite IHn'. rewrite Nat.add_succ_r. reflexivity.
Qed.
exposition_text: {"summary":"This exposition walks through the proof that addition of natural numbers is commutative (n + m = m + n), using structural induction on the first argument. We visualize the inductive structure, the key rewriting steps, and the algebraic relationships involved.","visualizations":[{"type":"text","title":"Introduction","description":"Overview of the commutativity proof","data":{"content":"The commutativity of natural number addition ($n + m = m + n$) is one of the most fundamental properties in arithmetic. While it seems obvious, proving it formally requires careful induction because addition is defined recursively. In Coq (Rocq), addition is defined by recursion on the first argument: - $0 + m = m$ (by definition) - $S(n') + m = S(n' + m)$ (by definition) Notice that these rules only simplify the left argument. To prove $n + m = m + n$, we must show that the right argument behaves symmetrically — which requires induction."}},{"type":"commutative_diagram","title":"Proof Structure","description":"The inductive skeleton: base case and inductive step as a commutative diagram of equalities","data":{"objects":[{"id":"goal","label":"n + m = m + n","row":0,"col":1},{"id":"base","label":"0 + m = m + 0","row":1,"col":0},{"id":"step","label":"S n' + m = m + S n'","row":1,"col":2},{"id":"base_lhs","label":"m","row":2,"col":0},{"id":"step_lhs","label":"S(n' + m)","row":2,"col":2},{"id":"step_rhs","label":"S(m + n')","row":3,"col":2}],"morphisms":[{"source":"goal","target":"base","label":"n = 0","style":"solid"},{"source":"goal","target":"step","label":"n = S n'","style":"solid"},{"source":"base","target":"base_lhs","label":"simpl + add_0_r","style":"dashed"},{"source":"step","target":"step_lhs","label":"simpl","style":"dashed"},{"source":"step_lhs","target":"step_rhs","label":"IH: n'+m = m+n'","style":"solid"},{"source":"step","target":"step_rhs","label":"add_succ_r","style":"dashed"}]}},{"type":"text","title":"Base Case","description":"Walking through n = 0","data":{"content":"When $n = 0$, the goal becomes $0 + m = m + 0$. The left side simplifies immediately by the definition of addition: $0 + m \to m$. For the right side, $m + 0 = m$ is not definitional — it requires the lemma $\texttt{Nat.add\_0\_r}$, which itself is proved by induction on $m$. After rewriting, both sides are $m$, and $\texttt{reflexivity}$ closes the goal."}},{"type":"generic_graph","title":"Rewriting Chain (Inductive Step)","description":"The chain of equalities in the inductive step: S n' + m = S(n' + m) = S(m + n') = m + S n'","data":{"nodes":[{"id":"e1","label":"S n' + m","group":"lhs"},{"id":"e2","label":"S(n' + m)","group":"rewrite"},{"id":"e3","label":"S(m + n')","group":"rewrite"},{"id":"e4","label":"m + S n'","group":"rhs"}],"edges":[{"source":"e1","target":"e2","label":"simpl","directed":true},{"source":"e2","target":"e3","label":"IH","directed":true},{"source":"e3","target":"e4","label":"add_succ_r","directed":true}]}},{"type":"text","title":"Inductive Step","description":"Detailed walkthrough of n = S n'","data":{"content":"Assuming the inductive hypothesis $\text{IH}: n' + m = m + n'$, we must prove $S(n') + m = m + S(n')$. The proof proceeds in three rewrites: 1. **simpl**: By definition, $S(n') + m$ computes to $S(n' + m)$. 2. **rewrite IH**: The inductive hypothesis lets us replace $n' + m$ with $m + n'$, giving $S(m + n')$. 3. **rewrite add_succ_r**: The library lemma $\texttt{Nat.add\_succ\_r}$ states $m + S(n') = S(m + n')$. Rewriting right-to-left transforms our goal $S(m + n')$ into $m + S(n')$, which matches the target. $\texttt{reflexivity}$ finishes the proof."}},{"type":"table","title":"Key Lemmas Used","description":"Library lemmas required for the proof","data":{"headers":["Lemma","Statement","Role in Proof"],"rows":[["Nat.add_0_r","m + 0 = m","Closes the base case (right identity)"],["Nat.add_succ_r","m + S n' = S(m + n')","Final rewrite in the inductive step"],["IH (induction)","n' + m = m + n'","Bridges lhs to rhs in inductive step"]],"caption":"The proof relies on two standard library lemmas plus the induction hypothesis."}},{"type":"region_diagram","title":"Proof Landscape","description":"How the proof components relate: definitions, lemmas, and the induction principle","data":{"regions":[{"id":"nat","label":"Natural Numbers","cx":250,"cy":250,"r":200,"color":"#58a6ff"},{"id":"add_def","label":"Addition (recursive def)","cx":180,"cy":200,"r":80,"color":"#7ee787"},{"id":"ind","label":"Induction Principle","cx":340,"cy":200,"r":80,"color":"#d2a8ff"},{"id":"comm","label":"Commutativity","cx":250,"cy":340,"r":60,"color":"#f0883e"}],"labels":[{"text":"0 + m = m","x":140,"y":180},{"text":"S n' + m = S(n'+m)","x":180,"y":230},{"text":"P(0) and P(n) => P(S n)","x":340,"y":210},{"text":"n + m = m + n","x":250,"y":345}]}}]}
title: Why n + m = m + n: Commutativity of Addition
summary: A visual walkthrough of the inductive proof that natural number addition is commutative, showing the proof structure, rewriting chains, and key lemmas.
